Party_Details.Name: TYC Investment Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: TYC Investment Pte Ltd was established as a family holding company
TYC entered into a deed with HT and JC on 2012-06-11 in respect of certain matters relating to TYC
An extraordinary general meeting was held to address JC's refusal to approve payments
HT and JC agreed that both must approve payments by TYC
HT informed TYC's shareholders of TSMP's appointment by email

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a company can enforce payment obligations against a director who refuses to approve payments under a mutual agreement
Party_Details.Rule: A director must act in the best interest of the company and cannot withhold approval for payments capriciously or arbitrarily
Party_Details.Application: JC' her refusal was justified due to concerns over the legitimacy of the expenses
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that while JC had some valid concerns, her refusal to approve payments was not entirely justified under the agreed terms.

Party_Details.Name: Jannie Chan Siew Lee

Facts.Text: Jannie Chan Siew Lee was one of TYC's permanent Governing Directors under article 8 of its articles of association
JC refused to approve certain payments by TYC
JC entered into the TYC Deed with HT and TYC in respect of certain matters relating to TYC as agreed in the DOS and the SSD
JC informed HT and TSMP that she did not recognize TSMP's authority to act for TYC
JC did not attend the extraordinary general meeting called by HT
JC refused to approve payment of KPMG's invoice for $19,424.05

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director can be compelled to approve payments when there is a deadlock in management decisions
Party_Details.Rule: A director's refusal to approve payments must be justified and not contrary to the company's best interests
Party_Details.Application: Her refusal was based on legitimate concerns about the expenses. JC's actions were obstructive and against the agreed terms
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court acknowledged JC's concerns but emphasized the need for cooperation in management decisions.

Party_Details.Name: Tay Yun Chwan Henry

Facts.Text: HT is a founder of THG and a permanent Governing Director of TYC[cite: 3408, 3416].
HT’s private company, Amstay Pte Ltd, paid KPMG fees that JC refused to approve, seeking reimbursement from TYC[cite: 3504].
HT called and attended the EGM to pass resolutions aimed at dealing with JC's 'obstructive conduct'[cite: 3535, 3536].

Party_Details.Issue: Whether a director can be authorized by shareholders to unilaterally sign cheques, bypassing a contractually mandated joint-approval system[cite: 3950].
Party_Details.Rule: Shareholder reserve powers cannot be used to contravene an express term in a company’s articles or rewrite the rights/obligations of the parties under a settlement deed[cite: 3826, 3957].
Party_Details.Application: The EGM's attempt to authorize HT to sign cheques unilaterally was a breach of the Payment Clause, which required both directors' signatures[cite: 3952].
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: Although HT was aligned with the company’s effort to break the deadlock, his attempt to assume unilateral payment authority was rejected as it would re-write the SSD[cite: 3614, 3959]. HT was ordered to bear his own costs[cite: 4270].
